section_policy.guidance: After the title, overview, and table of contents, choose feature-specific topic sections. Do not use a fixed section template for every feature. Sections should explain meaningful usage themes rather than list APIs mechanically.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Create and configure the primary object using public dali-ui APIs.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Prefer typed constructors and setters when available.

usage_patterns.intent: React to user or resource events through public signals.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use signal connection APIs only with their owning trait or class.

rendered_prompt:
You are generating a DALi dali-ui application developer guide.
Return only the complete Markdown document. Do not include commentary outside Markdown.

Hard requirements:
- Write the final user-facing guide in en only.
- Do not include Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose in the final guide.
- Assume dali-ui is the default application layer.
- Use Dali::Ui::View as the app-facing object model instead of raw Dali::Actor.
- Use only API symbols from the provided Allowed symbols, Property owners, and Usage patterns.
- Explain primary/supporting symbols as owned content when their usage scope says so.
- Use context/reference symbols only for setup, examples, or background lookup.
- Do not invent DALi APIs.
- Do not use raw Actor/SetProperty idioms unless the context pack allows it.
- If you are uncertain about an API, omit that example or use another provided symbol. Do not write unresolved placeholders.
- Do not expose prompt instructions, schema names, uncertainty notes, or validation comments in the user document.
- Start with a feature title, then a very short overview, then a table of contents.
- Create feature-specific topic sections rather than a fixed template.
- Include rich C++ API usage examples and use backticks for API names.
- Prefer concise, compilable-looking snippets over long sample copies.

Source grounding and API verification:
- Write enough practical detail for application developers to use the feature in a dali-ui app.
- Ground natural-language behavior claims in the actual public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when the context pack is not enough.
- Before writing examples, inspect the referenced public headers and relevant sample files when needed.
- Prefer examples copied or adapted from existing samples when they match the feature; otherwise keep examples small and verify every API use.
- After drafting, review the completed Markdown line by line against repository evidence and fix inaccurate explanations or code before returning.
- For every C++ code block, inline API statement, and backticked API name, verify:
  - receiver method exists on the receiver type
  - static methods are called statically and member methods are called on valid instances
  - assignment target is compatible with the method return type
  - argument count, argument type, and overload shape match the public API
  - enum owner and enum value both exist
  - property constants use the correct owner and value type
  - signal callback signatures match the public signal typedef
  - code blocks do not rely on inferred namespaces or overloads from naming alone

Document target:
- Feature: Lottie Animation View (lottie-animation-view)
- Category: views-components
- Guide profile: dali-ui-app
- Audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

Structure policy:
{
  "required_shell": [
    "title",
    "overview",
    "table_of_contents"
  ],
  "section_policy": {
    "fixed_sections": false,
    "guidance": "After the title, overview, and table of contents, choose feature-specific topic sections. Do not use a fixed section template for every feature. Sections should explain meaningful usage themes rather than list APIs mechanically.\n",
    "max_topic_sections": 8,
    "min_topic_sections": 2,
    "require_api_examples_per_topic": true,
    "require_backticked_api_names": true
  },
  "markdown": {
    "frontmatter": {
      "enabled": true,
      "fields": {
        "title": "{display_name}",
        "sidebar_label": "{display_name}",
        "category": "{category}"
      }
    },
    "heading_style": "atx",
    "code_fence_language": "cpp"
  },
  "content_quality": {
    "overview_style": "very_short",
    "require_table_of_contents": true,
    "require_rich_api_examples": true,
    "require_backticks_for_api_names": true,
    "topic_sections_must_be_feature_specific": true,
    "avoid_mechanical_api_listing": true,
    "default_object_model": "Use Dali::Ui::View rather than Dali::Actor for app-facing object examples.",
    "guide_assumption": "The reader is using dali-ui to build applications."
  }
}

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/layouts/stack-layout-params.h>
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/layouts/stack-layout.h>
#include <dali/integration-api/debug.h>

using namespace Dali;
using namespace Dali::Ui;

/**
 * ImageColor sample for ImageView / AnimatedImageView / LottieAnimationView:
 * - SetImageColor() applies an RGBA color as a per-pixel multiplier on the image
 * - WHITE (0xFFFFFF) means no tint — the image appears as original
 * - Preset buttons demonstrate: no tint, red/green/blue channels, desaturate (gray), dim
 * - Alpha column shows partial transparency via the alpha component
 * - IMAGE TYPE button switches between ImageView, AnimatedImageView (GIF), and LottieAnimationView
 * - Press Escape or Back to quit
 */
class ImageColorController : public ConnectionTracker
{
  static constexpr int COLOR_COUNT = 6;
  static constexpr int IMAGE_TYPE_COUNT = 3;

  struct ColorEntry
  {
    const char* name;
```

- repos/dali-ui/samples/image-view/image-loading-policy-example.cpp:53 (sample, score=1.0)
```cpp
* - One image at a time; CHANGE cycles through images for the current type
 * - PLAY / STOP for animated types
 * - SYNC LOADING toggle
 * - FAST TRACK UPLOADING toggle (not applicable to Lottie)
 * - RELEASE POLICY: DETACHED / DESTROYED / NEVER with HIDE 1s test
 * - LOAD POLICY: ATTACHED / IMMEDIATE with TEST (loads before scene add)
 * - Press Escape or Back to quit
 */
class ImageLoadingPolicyController : public ConnectionTracker
{
  enum class ViewType { IMAGE = 0, ANIMATED, LOTTIE, COUNT };
  static constexpr int TYPE_COUNT = static_cast<int>(ViewType::COUNT);

  struct ReleasePolicyEntry
  {
    const char*              label;
    Ui::Image::ReleasePolicy policy;
  };
  static const ReleasePolicyEntry RELEASE_POLICIES[3];

public:
  explicit ImageLoadingPolicyController(Application& application)
  : mApplication(application),
    mViewType(ViewType::IMAGE),
```
